expectations.text: Produces both deliverables — a markdown behavior spec (.md) and a Cucumber .feature file
expectations.evidence: Output dir contains only transaction-csv-export-spec.md; no .feature file was produced.

expectations.text: The .feature file is well-formed Gherkin (Feature declaration, Scenario blocks, Given/When/Then steps)
expectations.evidence: No .feature file exists. Gherkin appears inside plain ``` code blocks in the .md, and most blocks have no 'Feature:' wrapper.

expectations.text: Happy-path scenarios are tagged @happy-path and match the CSV export flow described in the prompt
expectations.evidence: 0 @happy-path tags. The happy-path scenario in Section 5.1 is untagged and not distinguishable from the deviation scenarios.

expectations.text: The spec includes a coverage checklist enumerating all 14 deviation taxonomy categories
expectations.evidence: No 14-category taxonomy checklist. Section 13 is an ad hoc acceptance checklist.

expectations.text: Deviation scenarios span at least 8 distinct deviation categories
expectations.evidence: Scenarios touch ~8 categories (invalid input, state conflict, boundary, duplicate/retry, external failure, auth/permission, environment, connectivity).

expectations.text: Deviation scenario Then-steps specify concrete graceful product behavior, not merely that an error occurred
expectations.evidence: Scenarios resolve gracefully (e.g. generation failure: no partial file, error message inviting retry, button restored).

expectations.text: Genuine product decisions are tagged @assumption and consolidated into a Flagged Assumptions section
expectations.evidence: 0 @assumption tags. Section 12 'Open questions' and inline 'Decision point' callouts raise decisions in prose but are untagged and not consolidated as a per-scenario review table.

expectations.text: The .md spec includes the narrative sections Problem & Intent, Actors, Glossary, Preconditions, and Scope
expectations.evidence: Has Summary, Goals/Non-goals, Actors and preconditions — but no Glossary section.

expectations.text: Deviation scenarios cover an empty date range, an invalid range (end before start), a large export, and connectivity loss mid-flow
expectations.evidence: 5.2 empty range, 6.1 start-after-end, 8.2 large export, 9.1 generation/network failure — all present.
